title: Lap-Grouped Learning-to-Rank Blend
summary: AUC rewards ordering, and the real decision is partly relative: on a given lap, only a few drivers are plausible pit candidates.
rationale: AUC rewards ordering, and the real decision is partly relative: on a given lap, only a few drivers are plausible pit candidates. Learning-to-rank is designed for grouped binary relevance and is a different bias than plain binary classification.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost ranker with qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber), label=PitNextLap, and the same feature set plus field-relative interactions. Blend its OOF score with the classifier rather than replacing the classifier. Test both rank:pairwise and rank:ndcg.
expected_effect: Sharper within-lap ordering of who pits next, which can improve leaderboard AUC even if absolute probabilities move little.
risk: Groups with zero or one positive produce weak pairwise signal; also avoid using future same-race information when building lap-group priors.
